req.title: Patient portal login
req.statement: A patient shall log in using SSO; failed attempts shall be rate-limited and notify the patient.

tests.name: naive_case
tests.executable:
# Pytest Tests for SSO Login with Rate Limiting and Notification

```python
"""
Test Suite: SSO Login with Rate Limiting and Patient Notification
Requirement: A patient shall log in using SSO; failed attempts shall be
             rate-limited and notify the patient.
"""

import time
from datetime import datetime, timedelta
from http import HTTPStatus
from unittest.mock import MagicMock, call, patch

import pytest


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Constants (mirror production configuration)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
MAX_FAILED_ATTEMPTS = 5          # attempts before rate-limit kicks in
RATE_LIMIT_WINDOW_SECONDS = 300  # 5-minute sliding window
LOCKOUT_DURATION_SECONDS = 900   # 15-minute lockout


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Fixtures
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

@pytest.fixture
def sso_client(app):
    """HTTP test client with SSO endpoints mounted."""
    return app.test_client()


@pytest.fixture
def app():
    """Create and configure the application for testing."""
    from myapp import create_app  # replace with real import
    app = create_app({"TESTING": True, "RATELIMIT_STORAGE_URL": "memory://"})
    yield app


@pytest.fixture
def valid_sso_token():
    return {
        "sub": "patient-uuid-001",
        "email": "jane.doe@example.com",
        "name": "Jane Doe",
        "iss": "https://sso.trusted-idp.example.com",
        "exp": int((datetime.utcnow() + timedelta(hours=1)).timestamp()),
        "iat": int(datetime.utcnow().timestamp()),
        "nonce": "secure-random-nonce-abc123",
    }


@pytest.fixture
def expired_sso_token(valid_sso_token):
    expired = valid_sso_token.copy()
    expired["exp"] = int((datetime.utcnow() - timedelta(hours=1)).timestamp())
    return expired


@pytest.fixture
def notification_service():
    with patch("myapp.services.notification.NotificationService") as mock_cls:
        instance = mock_cls.return_value
        instance.send_email = MagicMock(return_value=True)
        instance.send_sms = MagicMock(return_value=True)
        instance.send_in_app = MagicMock(return_value=True)
        yield instance


@pytest.fixture
def rate_limit_store():
    """In-memory store that mirrors the production Redis rate-limit store."""
    with patch("myapp.services.rate_limiter.RateLimiter") as mock_cls:
        instance = mock_cls.return_value
        instance.attempt_count = 0
        instance.is_locked = False

        def increment(patient_id):
            instance.attempt_count += 1
            return instance.attempt_count

        def check_locked(patient_id):
            return instance.is_locked

        instance.increment = MagicMock(side_effect=increment)
        instance.is_locked_out = MagicMock(side_effect=check_locked)
        instance.reset = MagicMock(side_effect=lambda pid: setattr(instance, "attempt_count", 0))
        yield instance


@pytest.fixture
def patient_repository():
    with patch("myapp.repositories.PatientRepository") as mock_cls:
        instance = mock_cls.return_value
        instance.find_by_sub = MagicMock(return_value={
            "id": "patient-uuid-001",
            "email": "jane.doe@example.com",
            "phone": "+15550001234",
            "name": "Jane Doe",
            "active": True,
        })
        yield instance


# ===========================================================================
# 1. SUCCESSFUL SSO LOGIN
# ===========================================================================

class TestSuccessfulSSOLogin:

    def test_valid_token_returns_200(self, sso_client, valid_sso_token):
        """A valid SSO token must result in a successful login response."""
        with patch("myapp.sso.verify_token", return_value=valid_sso_token):
            response = sso_client.post(
                "/auth/sso/callback",
                json={"id_token": "valid.jwt.token"},
            )
        assert response.status_code == HTTPStatus.OK

    def test_successful_login_returns_session_token(self, sso_client, valid_sso_token):
        """A successful login must return a session token for the patient."""
        with patch("myapp.sso.verify_token", return_value=valid_sso_token):
            response = sso_client.post(
                "/auth/sso/callback",
                json={"id_token": "valid.jwt.token"},
            )
        data = response.get_json()
        assert "session_token" in data
        assert data["session_token"]  # non-empty

    def test_successful_login_returns_patient_identity(
        self, sso_client, valid_sso_token, patient_repository
    ):
        """Successful login response must include patient identity fields."""
        with patch("myapp.sso.verify_token", return_value=valid_sso_token):
            response = sso_client.post(
                "/auth/sso/callback",
                json={"id_token": "valid.jwt.token"},
            )
        data = response.get_json()
        assert data["patient_id"] == valid_sso_token["sub"]
        assert data["email"] == valid_sso_token["email"]

    def test_successful_login_resets_failed_attempt_counter(
        self, sso_client, valid_sso_token, rate_limit_store
    ):
        """After a successful login the failed-attempt counter must be cleared."""
        rate_limit_store.attempt_count = 3  # simulate prior failures
        with patch("myapp.sso.verify_token", return_value=valid_sso_token):
            sso_client.post(
                "/auth/sso/callback",
                json={"id_token": "valid.jwt.token"},
            )
        rate_limit_store.reset.assert_called_once_with(valid_sso_token["sub"])

    def test_sso_redirect_initiates_idp_flow(self, sso_client):
        """GET /auth/sso/login must redirect the patient to the IdP."""
        response = sso_client.get("/auth/sso/login")
        assert response.status_code in (HTTPStatus.FOUND, HTTPStatus.TEMPORARY_REDIRECT)
        location = response.headers.get("Location", "")
        assert "sso.trusted-idp.example.com" in location

    def test_sso_redirect_contains_state_and_nonce(self, sso_client):
        """The IdP redirect URL must include CSRF state and nonce parameters."""
        response = sso_client.get("/auth/sso/login")
        location = response.headers.get("Location", "")
        assert "state=" in location
        assert "nonce=" in location


# ===========================================================================
# 2. FAILED SSO LOGIN – TOKEN VALIDATION
# ===========================================================================

class TestFailedSSOLoginTokenValidation:

    def test_missing_token_returns_400(self, sso_client):
        """A request with no id_token must be rejected with 400 Bad Request."""
        response = sso_client.post("/auth/sso/callback", json={})
        assert response.status_code == HTTPStatus.BAD_REQUEST

    def test_malformed_token_returns_401(self, sso_client):
        """A malformed / unparseable JWT must be rejected with 401."""
        with patch("myapp.sso.verify_token", side_effect=ValueError("bad token")):
            response = sso_client.post(
                "/auth/sso/callback",
                json={"id_token": "not.a.valid.jwt"},
            )
        assert response.status_code == HTTPStatus.UNAUTHORIZED

    def test_expired_token_returns_401(self, sso_client, expired_sso_token):
        """An expired token must be rejected with 401."""
        with patch("myapp.sso.verify_token", side_effect=Exception("Token expired")):
            response = sso_client.post(
                "/auth/sso/callback",
                json={"id_token": "expired.jwt.token"},
            )
        assert response.status_code == HTTPStatus.UNAUTHORIZED

    def test_wrong_issuer_returns_401(self, sso_client, valid_sso_token):
        """A token from an untrusted issuer must be rejected with 401."""
        tampered = valid_sso_token.copy()
        tampered["iss"] = "https://evil.attacker.example.com"
        with patch("myapp.sso.verify_token", side_effect=Exception("Invalid issuer")):
            response = sso_client.post(
                "/auth/sso/callback",
                json={"id_token": "tampered.jwt.token"},
            )
        assert response.status_code == HTTPStatus.UNAUTHORIZED

    def test_tampered_signature_returns_401(self, sso_client):
        """A token whose signature does not verify must be rejected with 401."""
        with patch("myapp.sso.verify_token", side_effect=Exception("Signature verification failed")):
            response = sso_client.post(
                "/auth/sso/callback",
                json={"id_token": "header.payload.badsignature"},
            )
        assert response.status_code == HTTPStatus.UNAUTHORIZED

    def test_replayed_nonce_returns_401(self, sso_client, valid_sso_token):
        """A previously-used nonce must be rejected to prevent replay attacks."""
        with patch("myapp.sso.verify_token", side_effect=Exception("Nonce already used")):
            response = sso_client.post(
                "/auth/sso/callback",
                json={"id_token": "replay.jwt.token"},
            )
        assert response.status_code == HTTPStatus.UNAUTHORIZED

    def test_inactive_patient_returns_403(
        self, sso_client, valid_sso_token, patient_repository
    ):
        """A valid token for a deactivated patient account must return 403."""
        patient_repository.find_by_sub.return_value["active"] = False
        with patch("myapp.sso.verify_token", return_value=valid_sso_token):
            response = sso_client.post(
                "/auth/sso/callback",
                json={"id_token": "valid.jwt.token"},
            )
        assert response.status_code == HTTPStatus.FORBIDDEN


# ===========================================================================
# 3. RATE LIMITING – THRESHOLD ENFORCEMENT
# ===========================================================================

class TestRateLimiting:

    def _attempt_login(self, sso_client, times=1, token="bad.jwt.token"):
        """Helper: send `times` failed login attempts."""
        responses = []
        with patch("myapp.sso.verify_token", side_effect=Exception("Auth failed")):
            for _ in range(times):
                r = sso_client.post(
                    "/auth/sso/callback",
                    json={"id_token": token},
                    headers={"X-Patient-Id": "patient-uuid-001"},
                )
                responses.append(r)
        return responses

    def test_below_threshold_returns_401_not_429(self, sso_client):
        """Attempts below the limit must return 401, not 429."""
        responses = self._attempt_login(sso_client, times=MAX_FAILED_ATTEMPTS - 1)
        for r in responses:
            assert r.status_code == HTTPStatus.UNAUTHORIZED

    def test_threshold_attempt_returns_429(self, sso_client):
        """The attempt that reaches the limit must return 429 Too Many Requests."""
        responses = self._attempt_login(sso_client, times=MAX_FAILED_ATTEMPTS)
        assert responses[-1].status_code == HTTPStatus.TOO_MANY_REQUESTS

    def test_post_lockout_attempts_return_429(self, sso_client):
        """All requests during the lockout window must return 429."""
        self._attempt_login(sso_client, times=MAX_FAILED_ATTEMPTS)
        # additional attempt while locked
        extra_responses = self._attempt_login(sso_client, times=3)
        for r in extra_responses:
            assert r.status_code == HTTPStatus.TOO_MANY_REQUESTS

    def test_rate_limit_response_contains_retry_after_header(self, sso_client):
        """A 429 response must include a Retry-After header."""
        responses = self._attempt_login(sso_client, times=MAX_FAILED_ATTEMPTS)
        assert "Retry-After" in responses[-1].headers

    def test_retry_after_value_is_correct(self, sso_client):
        """Retry-After must equal the configured lockout duration."""
        responses = self._attempt_login(sso_client, times=MAX_FAILED_ATTEMPTS)
        retry_after = int(responses[-1].headers["Retry-After"])
        assert retry_after == LOCKOUT_DURATION_SECONDS

    def test_rate_limit_response_body_indicates_lockout(self, sso_client):
        """The 429 body must include a human-readable lockout message."""
        responses = self._attempt_login(sso_client, times=MAX_FAILED_ATTEMPTS)
        data = responses[-1].get_json()
        assert "error" in data
        assert data["error"] in (
            "account_locked",
            "too_many_attempts",
            "rate_limited",
        )

    def test_rate_limit_is_per_patient_not_global(self, sso_client):
        """Rate-limiting must be scoped per patient, not across all users."""
        # Exhaust patient-001's limit
        with patch("myapp.sso.verify_token", side_effect=Exception("Auth failed")):
            for _ in range(MAX_FAILED_ATTEMPTS):
                sso_client.post(
                    "/auth/sso/callback",
                    json={"id_token": "bad"},
                    headers={"X-Patient-Id": "patient-uuid-001"},
                )
        # patient-002 should still receive 401, not 429
        with patch("myapp.sso.verify_token", side_effect=Exception("Auth failed")):
            response = sso_client.post(
                "/auth/sso/callback",
                json={"id_token": "bad"},
                headers={"X-Patient-Id": "patient-uuid-002"},
            )
        assert response.status_code == HTTPStatus.UNAUTHORIZED

    def test_rate_limit_resets_after_lockout_window(self, sso_client):
        """After the lockout window expires the patient can attempt login again."""
        self._attempt_login(sso_client, times=MAX_FAILED_ATTEMPTS)

        future_time = datetime.utcnow() + timedelta(seconds=LOCKOUT_DURATION_SECONDS + 1)
        with patch("myapp.sso.verify_token", side_effect=Exception("Auth failed")), \
             patch("myapp.utils.time.utcnow", return_value=future_time):
            response = sso_client.post(
                "/auth/sso/callback",
                json={"id_token": "bad"},
                headers={"X-Patient-Id": "patient-uuid-001"},
            )
        # Should be 401 (auth failure) rather than 429 (still locked)
        assert response.status_code == HTTPStatus.UNAUTHORIZED

    def test_successful_login_not_counted_toward_rate_limit(
        self, sso_client, valid_sso_token, rate_limit_store
    ):
        """Successful logins must not increment the failed-attempt counter."""
        with patch("myapp.sso.verify_token", return_value=valid_sso_token):
            sso_client.post("/auth/sso/callback", json={"id_token": "good.jwt"})
        rate_limit_store.increment.assert_not_called()


# ===========================================================================
# 4. PATIENT NOTIFICATION ON FAILED ATTEMPTS
# ===========================================================================

class TestPatientNotificationOnFailure:

    def test_first_failed_attempt_sends_notification(
        self, sso_client, notification_service
    ):
        """The patient must be notified on the very first failed login attempt."""
        with patch("myapp.sso.verify_token", side_effect=Exception("Auth failed")):
            sso_client.post(
                "/auth/sso/callback",
                json={"id_token": "bad.token"},
                headers={"X-Patient-Id": "patient-uuid-001"},
            )
        assert (
            notification_service.send_email.called
            or notification_service.send_sms.called
            or notification_service.send_in_app.called
        )

    def test_failed_login_email_sent_to_correct_address(
        self, sso_client, notification_service, patient_repository
    ):
        """Failure notification email must be sent to the patient's registered address."""
        expected_email = patient_repository.find_by_sub.return_value["email"]
        with patch("myapp.sso.verify_token", side_effect=Exception("Auth failed")):
            sso_client.post(
                "/auth/sso/callback",
                json={"id_token": "bad.token"},
                headers={"X-Patient-Id": "patient-uuid-001"},
            )
        call_kwargs = notification_service.send_email.call_args
        assert call_kwargs is not None
        assert call_kwargs.kwargs.get("to") == expected_email or \
               call_kwargs.args[0] == expected_email

    def test_lockout_notification_sent_when_threshold_reached(
        self, sso_client, notification_service
    ):
        """A distinct lockout notification must be sent when the limit is hit."""
        with patch("myapp.sso.verify_token", side_effect=Exception("Auth failed")):
            for _ in range(MAX_FAILED_ATTEMPTS):
                sso_client.post(
                    "/auth/sso/callback",
                    json={"id_token": "bad.token"},
                    headers={"X-Patient-Id": "patient-uuid-001"},
                )
        # Find the lockout-specific call
        email_calls = notification_service.send_email.call_args_list
        lockout_calls = [
            c for c in email_calls
            if "locked" in str(c).lower() or "lockout" in str(c).lower()
        ]
        assert len(lockout_calls) >= 1, "Expected at least one lockout notification email"

    def test_lockout_notification_contains_unlock_instructions(
        self, sso_client, notification_service
    ):
        """The lockout notification must include instructions for regaining access."""
        with patch("myapp.sso.verify_token", side_effect=Exception("Auth failed")):
            for _ in range(MAX_FAILED_ATTEMPTS):
                sso_client.post(
                    "/auth/sso/callback",
                    json={"id_token": "bad.token"},
                    headers={"X-Patient-Id": "patient-uuid-001"},
                )
        all_calls_str = str(notification_service.send_email.call_args_list)
        # Unlock / support instructions should be mentioned
        assert any(
            keyword in all_calls_str.lower()
            for keyword in ["unlock", "support", "contact", "retry", "minutes"]
        )

    def test_notification_not_sent_on_successful_login(
        self, sso_client, valid_sso_token, notification_service
    ):
        """No failure notification must be dispatched when login succeeds."""
        with patch("myapp.sso.verify_token", return_value=valid_sso_token):
            sso_client.post(
                "/auth/sso/callback",
                json={"id_token": "valid.jwt.token"},
            )
        notification_service.send_email.assert_not_called()
        notification_service.send_sms.assert_not_called()

    def test_notification_not_duplicated_for_every_post_lockout_attempt(
        self, sso_client, notification_service
    ):
        """After lockout, further attempts must not re-send the lockout notification."""
        with patch("myapp.sso.verify_token", side_effect=Exception("Auth failed")):
            for _ in range(MAX_FAILED_ATTEMPTS + 5):  # 5 extra post-lockout attempts
                sso_client.post(
                    "/auth/sso/callback",
                    json={"id_token": "bad.token"},
                    headers={"X-Patient-Id": "patient-uuid-001"},
                )
        # Total email calls must not exceed MAX_FAILED_ATTEMPTS (one per failure up to lockout)
        assert notification_service.send_email.call_count <= MAX_FAILED_ATTEMPTS

    def test_notification_includes_timestamp_of_failed_attempt(
        self, sso_client, notification_service
    ):
        """The notification payload must include the timestamp of the failure."""
        with patch("myapp.sso.verify_token", side_effect=Exception("Auth failed")):
            sso_client.post(
                "/auth/sso/callback",
                json={"id_token": "bad.token"},
                headers={"X-Patient-Id": "patient-uuid-001"},
            )
        call_kwargs = notification_service.send_email.call_args
        notification_body = str(call_kwargs)
        assert any(
            keyword in notification_body.lower()
            for keyword in ["timestamp", "time", "utc", "at "]
        )

    def test_notification_includes_ip_address_in_alert(
        self, sso_client, notification_service
    ):
        """Security notifications should include the originating IP address."""
        with patch("myapp.sso.verify_token", side_effect=Exception("Auth failed")):
            sso_client.post(
                "/auth/sso/callback",
                json={"id_token": "bad.token"},
                headers={
                    "X-Patient-Id": "patient-uuid-001",
                    "X-Forwarded-For": "203.0.113.42",
                },
            )
        call_kwargs_str = str(notification_service.send_email.call_args)
        assert "203.0.113.42" in call_kwargs_str


# ===========================================================================
# 5. EDGE CASES AND SECURITY HARDENING
# ===========================================================================

class TestEdgeCasesAndSecurity:

    def test_concurrent_requests_do_not_exceed_rate_limit_counter(
        self, app, valid_sso_token
    ):
        """Concurrent failed attempts must not bypass the atomic rate-limit counter."""
        import threading

        results = []

        def attempt():
            client = app.test_client()
            with patch("myapp.sso.verify_token", side_effect=Exception("Auth failed")):
                r = client.post(
                    "/auth/sso/callback",
                    json={"id_token": "bad.token"},
                    headers={"X-Patient-Id": "patient-uuid-001"},
                )
                results.append(r.status_code)

        threads = [threading.Thread(target=attempt) for _ in range(MAX_FAILED_ATTEMPTS + 5)]
        for t in threads:
            t.start()
        for t in threads:
            t.join()

        too_many = results.count(HTTPStatus.TOO_MANY_REQUESTS)
        assert too_many >= 1, "At least some concurrent requests should be rate-limited"

    def test_response_does_not_leak_internal_error_details(self, sso_client):
        """Error responses must not expose stack traces or internal details."""
        with patch("myapp.sso.verify_token", side_effect=Exception("DB connection error")):
            response = sso_client.post(
                "/auth/sso/callback",
                json={"id_token": "bad.token"},
            )
        body = response.get_data(as_text=True)
        assert "Traceback" not in body
        assert "DB connection" not in body

    def test_no_account_enumeration_via_error_message(self, sso_client):
        """Error messages for unknown vs known patients must be identical."""
        with patch("myapp.sso.verify_token", side_effect=Exception("Auth failed")), \
             patch("myapp.repositories.PatientRepository.find_by_sub", return_value=None):
            r_unknown = sso_client.post(
                "/auth/sso/callback",
                json={"id_token": "unknown.patient.token"},
            )

        with patch("myapp.sso.verify_token", side_effect=Exception("Auth failed")):
            r_known = sso_client.post(
                "/auth/sso/callback",
                json={"id_token": "known.patient.bad.token"},
            )

        assert r_unknown.status_code == r_known.status_code
        assert r_unknown.get_json().get("error") == r_known.get_json().get("error")

    def test_state_parameter_validated_on_callback(self, sso_client):
        """The callback must reject requests with a mismatched CSRF state value."""
        response = sso_client.post(
            "/auth/sso/callback",
            json={"id_token": "valid.token", "state": "tampered-state-value"},
        )
        assert response.status_code in (HTTPStatus.BAD_REQUEST, HTTPStatus.UNAUTHORIZED)

    def test_session_token_not_returned_when_rate_limited(self, sso_client):
        """A 429 response must never contain a session token."""
        with patch("myapp.sso.verify_token", side_effect=Exception("Auth failed")):
            for _ in range(MAX_FAILED_ATTEMPTS):
                sso_client.post(
                    "/auth/sso/callback",
                    json={"id_token": "bad.token"},
                    headers={"X-Patient-Id": "patient-uuid-001"},
                )
            response = sso_client.post(
                "/auth/sso/callback",
                json={"id_token": "bad.token"},
                headers={"X-Patient-Id": "patient-uuid-001"},
            )
        assert response.status_code == HTTPStatus.TOO_MANY_REQUESTS
        data = response.get_json()
        assert "session_token" not in data

    def test_https_enforced_on_callback_endpoint(self, sso_client):
        """The SSO callback must reject plain HTTP requests."""
        response = sso_client.post(
            "/auth/sso/callback",
            json={"id_token": "token"},
            base_url="http://localhost",
        )
        assert response.status_code in (
            HTTPStatus.BAD_REQUEST,
            HTTPStatus.FORBIDDEN,
            HTTPStatus.MOVED_PERMANENTLY,
        )
```

---

## Design Notes

| Area | Decision |
|---|---|
| **Token validation** | Tests cover expiry, bad issuer, tampered signature, and nonce replay — the main OIDC attack vectors |
| **Rate-limit granularity** | Per-patient, not per-IP, to avoid penalising patients on shared networks |
| **Notifications** | Verified for content (recipient, timestamp, IP, unlock instructions) not just that they fire |
| **Security hardening** | Enumeration prevention, no internal leakage, HTTPS enforcement, CSRF state check |
| **Concurrency** | Thread-safety test guards against TOCTOU races on the counter |
| **Fixture isolation** | All external dependencies (DB, notification service, rate-limit store) are mocked so tests are hermetic |

doc._raw_response:
# Pytest Tests for SSO Login with Rate Limiting and Notification

```python
"""
Test Suite: SSO Login with Rate Limiting and Patient Notification
Requirement: A patient shall log in using SSO; failed attempts shall be
             rate-limited and notify the patient.
"""

import time
from datetime import datetime, timedelta
from http import HTTPStatus
from unittest.mock import MagicMock, call, patch

import pytest


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Constants (mirror production configuration)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
MAX_FAILED_ATTEMPTS = 5          # attempts before rate-limit kicks in
RATE_LIMIT_WINDOW_SECONDS = 300  # 5-minute sliding window
LOCKOUT_DURATION_SECONDS = 900   # 15-minute lockout


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Fixtures
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

@pytest.fixture
def sso_client(app):
    """HTTP test client with SSO endpoints mounted."""
    return app.test_client()


@pytest.fixture
def app():
    """Create and configure the application for testing."""
    from myapp import create_app  # replace with real import
    app = create_app({"TESTING": True, "RATELIMIT_STORAGE_URL": "memory://"})
    yield app


@pytest.fixture
def valid_sso_token():
    return {
        "sub": "patient-uuid-001",
        "email": "jane.doe@example.com",
        "name": "Jane Doe",
        "iss": "https://sso.trusted-idp.example.com",
        "exp": int((datetime.utcnow() + timedelta(hours=1)).timestamp()),
        "iat": int(datetime.utcnow().timestamp()),
        "nonce": "secure-random-nonce-abc123",

"""
Test Suite: SSO Login with Rate Limiting and Patient Notification
Requirement: A patient shall log in using SSO; failed attempts shall be
             rate-limited and notify the patient.
"""

import time
from datetime import datetime, timedelta
from http import HTTPStatus
from unittest.mock import MagicMock, call, patch

import pytest


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Constants (mirror production configuration)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
MAX_FAILED_ATTEMPTS = 5          # attempts before rate-limit kicks in
RATE_LIMIT_WINDOW_SECONDS = 300  # 5-minute sliding window
LOCKOUT_DURATION_SECONDS = 900   # 15-minute lockout

# Fixtures
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

@pytest.fixture
def rate_limit_store():
    """In-memory store that mirrors the production Redis rate-limit store."""
    with patch("myapp.services.rate_limiter.RateLimiter") as mock_cls:
        instance = mock_cls.return_value
        instance.attempt_count = 0
        instance.is_locked = False

        def increment(patient_id):
            instance.attempt_count += 1
            return instance.attempt_count

        def check_locked(patient_id):
            return instance.is_locked

        instance.increment = MagicMock(side_effect=increment)
        instance.is_locked_out = MagicMock(side_effect=check_locked)
        instance.reset = MagicMock(side_effect=lambda pid: setattr(instance, "attempt_count", 0))
        yield instance
